commit.message:
fix: space permissions not strict enough

Spaces lacked role enforcement — any member could invite, remove users,
or delete a space, and owners could leave their own space.

## Permission changes

- **Owner only**: Invite user, Remove user, Delete space
- **Non-owner only**: Leave space (owners cannot leave)
- Personal space: unchanged (invite shown, no leave/delete)

## Owner visibility

- Active space name row shows an "Owner" badge
- Switch-space list appends `· Owner` to spaces the current user owns
(both iOS sheet and Android action menu)

## Implementation

`isOwner` computed once in `useSpaceManagement`:
```ts
const isOwner = activeSpace?.ownerId === getUserId();
```

Propagated to `SpaceActions`, `UserList` (`canRemove` prop),
`SpaceNameRow`, and `buildLabel`. Applied consistently in both
`SpaceSheet` (iOS) and `SpaceManagementScreen`.

---------

fix: hideTitleForThumbnailIcons has no effect (#779) `hideTitleForThumbnailIcons: true` stopped working because `renderMediaGridCard` always fell back to rendering a title when `titleContent` was `undefined` — which is exactly what `favorites-icons.ts` passed to suppress it. ## Changes - **`renderMediaGridCard` (`utils.ts`)**: Changed `titleContent` type from `TemplateResult | undefined` to `TemplateResult | typeof nothing | undefined`, and replaced `??` with an explicit `!== undefined` guard so `nothing` can suppress rendering without triggering the default fallback: ```ts // Before — undefined triggers fallback, title always renders ${titleContent ?? html`
${item.title}
`} // After — nothing passes through as an explicit "render nothing" ${titleContent !== undefined ? titleContent : html`
${item.title}
`} ``` - **`favorites-icons.ts`**: Pass `nothing` instead of `undefined` when `showTitle` is false, so the intent to suppress the title is preserved through `renderMediaGridCard`. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: punxaphil <26582898+punxaphil@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ADO PR mapping: use lastMergeCommit nested object instead of flat mergeCommitId (#150)

The Azure DevOps GitPullRequest API returns lastMergeCommit as a nested
GitCommitRef object (with commitId), not as a flat mergeCommitId string.
The previous code deserialized a non-existent mergeCommitId field, which
always returned null from real API calls, breaking commit-to-PR mapping
for all merge strategies (including squash merges).

- Add AzureDevOpsGitCommitRef record to model the nested commit reference
- Change AzureDevOpsPullRequest to deserialize LastMergeCommit (nested object)
- Add convenience MergeCommitId property to minimize downstream changes
- Update mock handler to emit lastMergeCommit: { commitId: ... } format

test: comprehensive unit test coverage boost (37% → 55%) with incremental coverage gate (#107)

- Add 70 tests for StreamingController (streaming/batch modes, segment

- Add 87 tests for GatewayServer (auth flow, request routing for all 24+

- Add 43 tests for WorktreeManager (branch detection, create/remove/merge

- Add 75 tests for GatewayWsClient (connect/disconnect/reconnect, RPC

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  serialization)
- Add 8 tests for TokenManager (caching, expiry with safety margin,
  concurrent deduplication, invalidation)
- Add 44 tests for WebhookServer (route matching with longest prefix,
  body parsing for JSON/URL-encoded/XML, 5MB limit, query params,
  error handling)
- Add 42 tests for TunnelManager (quick/named tunnel modes, cloudflared
  path detection, credential detection, unexpected exit callback,
  graceful shutdown)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  unhandled rejections

  (platformMessageId, currentTextPartId, textBuffer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(engines): add comprehensive adapter tests for Claude, Copilot, and OpenCode

- Claude adapter: 139 tests covering message handling, tool parts, permissions,
  text accumulation, stream error classification, session management
- Copilot adapter: 142 tests covering session lifecycle, message flow, event

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Raise global coverage thresholds from 15% to 50% (all 4 metrics now pass)
- Add incremental coverage check script (60% threshold for changed files)
- Add CI step to enforce incremental coverage on PRs
- Extend engine adapter tests (Claude +89, Codex converters +178, Copilot converters +53)
- Add new test suites: engine-manager (143), scheduled-task-service (115),

Overall: 60 test files, 2139 tests, coverage 37% → 55%

commit.message:
feat(loop): monitor PRs and repair legacy schema (#387)

This pull request introduces a robust system for monitoring the status
of GitHub pull requests associated with "pushed" loops, automatically
updating their state when a PR is merged. It adds a new
`pull_request_monitoring` field to the `loops` table, ensures
compatibility with legacy databases, and implements a background monitor
to periodically check and update PR status. The changes also include
comprehensive migration and regression tests to ensure reliability.

**Pull Request Monitoring Feature:**

* Added a new background service (`PushedLoopMonitor` in
`src/core/pushed-loop-monitor.ts`) that periodically checks the status
of GitHub PRs for "pushed" loops and auto-marks them as merged when
appropriate.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3f5bcd0b49bf1883446b31a3241a3e69a0dcaee90ffac8176298fe179217e25eR1-R183)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a2a171449d862fe29692ce031981047d7ab755ae7f84c707aef80701b3ea0c80R33)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a2a171449d862fe29692ce031981047d7ab755ae7f84c707aef80701b3ea0c80L192-R195)
* Implemented the `probePullRequestMonitoring` function in
`src/core/pull-request-navigation.ts` to interact with the GitHub CLI,
parse PR status, and map it to a structured monitoring state.

**Database Schema and Persistence:**

* Added a new `pull_request_monitoring` column to the `loops` table
through migration `v6`, with corresponding schema repair logic in
`ensureLoopSchema` to handle legacy databases with colliding migration
versions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3871aec464a6e19854797223be05fecaaa3eb57958cd15631131f39391d6d3adR230-R243)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-09ba9082eabb0c34e6551b213f4c38394e452aa20fa88d33b6b33eff83ef027bL504-R511)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R73)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R152)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-c5697fcd42ef59f5948ac51fd17b76bc96b7bd5805c1e93e591765f85567a7e6R314-R321)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-a54ff182c7e8acf56acfd6e4b9c3ff41e2c41a31c9b211b2deb9df75d9a478f9R522-R531)
* Updated the `LoopState` type and helpers to persist and load the new
monitoring state.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c16735135cfcf80ab949b21ef0a7f62f05d14b1aea0295233bed08b8c3185485R181-R183)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c16735135cfcf80ab949b21ef0a7f62f05d14b1aea0295233bed08b8c3185485R343-R360)

**Reliability and Testing:**

* Documented the migration collision trap and required test/fix patterns
in `AGENTS.md` to guide future schema changes and prevent legacy upgrade
issues.
* Added migration and regression tests to verify the new column is added
and repaired correctly in all scenarios.

**Bug Fixes and Minor Improvements:**

* Improved idempotency of loop merging logic to avoid redundant
transitions.
* Ensured `pullRequestMonitoring` is reset on push finalization to avoid
stale state.

These changes collectively enable reliable, automated tracking of PR
status for pushed loops, with strong backward compatibility and test
coverage.
